TITLE: Start where the community is. Build only what they need.
BODY: Goods on Country is a modular pathway from a practical need to community-held capability.

TITLE: The funder proposition
BODY: Fund a community-defined step, see the result, learn from it, and back the next step when the community asks.

TITLE: The Goods role
BODY: Help communities price, procure, deploy, operate, maintain, measure and tell the story of what they chose.

TITLE: 1. Listen
BODY: What does the community want to make possible now?

TITLE: 2. Scope
BODY: Define the smallest useful first step and a 90-day proof.

TITLE: 3. Assemble modules
BODY: Choose only the equipment, product, training, finance, logistics and story support that is useful now.

TITLE: 4. Price the capability
BODY: Equipment + place + people + production + continuity + enterprise + evidence + risk.

TITLE: 5. Deliver and learn
BODY: Install safely, train operators, run a real job, record cost, time, quality and issues.

TITLE: 6. Prove and return
BODY: Return an honest community-approved evidence pack to the community and funder.

TITLE: 7. Grow, pause or transfer
BODY: Keep it, add a module, share it, transfer more control, or stop if it is not useful.

TITLE: A. Listen, design and scope
BODY: Community brief, local asset map, pathway design and first proof.

TITLE: B. Product access and delivery
BODY: Stretch Beds, delivery, assembly, allocation, QR tracking and repair pathway.

TITLE: C. Collection and shred
BODY: Shredder, setup, safe operation, training, feedstock standard, maintenance and offtake.

TITLE: D. Assembly and repair hub
BODY: Benches, tools, spares, local assembly, maintenance and repair reporting.

TITLE: E. Press and make
BODY: Hot press, tooling, recipes, training, quality and measured first run.

TITLE: F. Cut and finish
BODY: CNC, toolpaths, fixtures, training, safety and quality.

TITLE: G. Full production facility
BODY: Integrated shred, press, cut, assemble and quality-control capability.

TITLE: H. Workforce and enterprise
BODY: Paid learning, operator roles, unit economics, buyer pathway and governance.

TITLE: I. Demand and finance
BODY: Committed demand, complete budget, capital stack, milestones and contracts.

TITLE: J. Story, evidence and learning
BODY: Consent-led photos, video, data, case study, deck and live map update.

TITLE: Jane at Utopia wants a shredder
BODY: Validate identity, authority, plastic stream, offtake, site and consent before external use.

TITLE: Shredder equipment
BODY: $15K-$30K, modelled pending vendor quotes.

TITLE: Do not fund a stranded machine
BODY: Confirm suitable feedstock, trained operator, safe site, maintenance and a reliable next use for the shredded plastic.

TITLE: The useful next piece
BODY: Jane did not ask for a factory. She asked for the useful next piece. The next decision belongs to Utopia.

TITLE: Nchumba wants the whole production facility
BODY: Confirm whether this means Oonchiumpa and validate the request before external use.

TITLE: Demand before capex
BODY: Current model gates major plant spend at roughly 300+ committed beds per year.

TITLE: Gross equipment
BODY: Shredder $15K-$30K + press $80K-$150K + CNC $15K-$40K + benches about $2K = $112K-$222K.

TITLE: Equipment is not the complete project
BODY: Add site works, freight, installation, safety, training, wages, working capital, first run, maintenance, governance, evidence and contingency.

TITLE: Measured 50-bed run
BODY: Target $60K-$80K to convert modelled cost, cycle time and quality into measured evidence.

TITLE: The return is the transfer
BODY: Equipment, knowledge, demand, margin and decisions moving together into community hands.

TITLE: Verified
BODY: Invoice, signed document or measured record.

TITLE: Workpaper
BODY: Checkable internal calculation.

TITLE: Modelled
BODY: Built from inputs, not yet demonstrated at this site.

TITLE: Target
BODY: Desired future state.

TITLE: Unknown
BODY: Requires a quote or community decision.

TITLE: Before
BODY: What was asked for, what was already strong and what constraint was in the way?

TITLE: Choice
BODY: Which modules did community choose, decline or defer?

TITLE: Build
BODY: What was funded, who did what, what did it cost and what changed?

TITLE: Proof
BODY: Output, paid participation, capability, cost, voice, problems and learning.

TITLE: Next
BODY: Keep, improve, add, share, transfer or stop.

TITLE: 1. Start where the community is
BODY: Community chooses the capability and pace.

TITLE: 2. One model, many entry points
BODY: Show the module menu from delivery to a full facility.

TITLE: 3. Two different asks
BODY: Place the shredder and full-facility pathways side by side.

TITLE: 4. Price capability, not a machine
BODY: Show the full cost stack and evidence labels.

TITLE: 5. Every project becomes proof
BODY: Show data, voice, photos, video and the next community-defined ask.

TITLE: 6. The invitation
BODY: Back the next community-defined step.

TITLE: Utopia delivery
BODY: First completed case study for delivery, local distribution, QR tracking and reporting.

TITLE: Consent
BODY: Do not use uncleared Utopia or Oonchiumpa media and quotes in external funder material.

TITLE: Validate community examples
BODY: Confirm Jane and "Nchumba"/Oonchiumpa details, mandate and permission.

TITLE: Get vendor quotes
BODY: Three quotes each for shredder, press and CNC.

TITLE: Build full-cost calculator
BODY: Separate equipment, complete project and ongoing operating cost.

TITLE: Finish one case study
BODY: Reconcile counts, clear media and produce the Utopia six-slide story.
